100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 22, 1926, the United States Senate began a crucial debate on the McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at providing price supports for struggling farmers, signaling growing tensions in agricultural policy. In Europe, a general strike in Britain reached its second week, with thousands of workers demanding better wages and working conditions amid economic unrest. Meanwhile, the political landscape in Germany continued to shift, as Chancellor Wilhelm Marx faced mounting challenges from both the left and right amid a struggling Weimar Republic.
Updated: Friday, May 22, 2026 at 09:05:17 AM CDT
